title: July 2026 release is live
summary: The July 2026 monthly GeoIP dataset is published across CSV, Parquet, and MMDB, with per-country packages plus ALL, bogon, government, and education special editions. Note: this month's city-level coordinates draw on the June DB-IP City Lite edition, as DB-IP had not yet released its July file at publication time.

title: Prefix Lookup: resolve any IPv4 block in your browser
summary: Look up any IPv4 address or /24 prefix and instantly see its published GeoIP location, ASN, WHOIS ownership, registry status, and risk signals - read directly from the monthly Parquet in your browser, with nothing sent to a server. Low-confidence blocks now show an explicit accuracy radius and an honest country-level label.

title: Interactive IPv4 Density and Region maps
summary: A hex-bin density heatmap showing where IPv4 address space concentrates worldwide, and a state/province (ISO 3166-2) choropleth of city-precise /24 footprint with click-to-zoom - both rendered entirely client-side, no map tiles, no tracking.

title: Spatially-validated coordinates and a live IANA TLD catalog
summary: Every published coordinate is validated against public-domain Natural Earth boundaries (about 99.97% country-level match) with standardized ISO 3166-2 region codes, and the TLD reference catalog now refreshes directly from the IANA Root Zone Database each release.

title: Open Parquet dataset on Hugging Face
summary: The full geolocated /24 set is published as an open, no-password Parquet dataset on Hugging Face under CC BY 4.0, refreshed every month alongside the site packages.

title: April 2026 release is ready
summary: The April 2026 monthly release is now available with refreshed CSV, Parquet, and MMDB package indexes.

title: MMDB packages added
summary: GeoIP Locations now publishes MMDB packages alongside CSV and Parquet for monthly IPv4 geolocation releases.

title: Large ALL packages published as release assets
summary: The ALL scope packages for CSV, Parquet, and MMDB are delivered through GitHub Release assets while country packages remain available through the release folder.

title: Download catalog expanded to three formats
summary: The monthly release catalog now separates CSV, Parquet, and MMDB package indexes so users can choose the format that best fits their workflow.
